cve_research_methodology.overview: Comprehensive methodology for researching CVEs using IBM Concert API plus authoritative external sources before implementing fixes. This is a CRITICAL FIRST STEP before any remediation work. ALWAYS use API endpoints instead of browser-based research for automation and accuracy. Concert is the required internal source of truth for application-specific exposure and package recommendations, and it must be cross-referenced with authoritative public vulnerability sources such as NVD.

critical_tool_usage_rules.rule: NEVER use web_fetch, browser_action, or any web browsing tools for CVE research
ALWAYS use execute_command with curl to query APIs (NVD, GitHub Advisory, OSV, Concert)
If an API call fails, document the failure and try alternative APIs - do NOT attempt browser-based research
Parse API responses using jq or grep to extract relevant information
critical_tool_usage_rules.rationale: Web browsing tools are unreliable in GitHub Actions environments and cannot be automated. API-based research with curl is fast, reliable, and produces structured data.

research_requirements.requirement: Always query IBM Concert API first to confirm the affected application, package exposure, and recommended remediation path.
Always cross-reference Concert findings with at least one authoritative public source, preferably NVD.
When package version guidance is available, compare Concert recommendations against public advisory data before selecting the final target version.
If Concert and external sources disagree, document the discrepancy and prefer the safer version that is both patched and supported by available evidence.
If Concert is temporarily unavailable, continue external research but explicitly note that Concert validation is still pending.

research_priority_order.title: Check Sources in This Order (Concert Plus Authoritative Cross-Reference)

source.name: IBM Concert API
source.authority: Highest for internal application context and package recommendations

extract.field: Application/build artifact context for the affected package

source.best_practice: Use Concert first to establish whether the target application is actually affected and what version path Concert recommends.

source.name: National Vulnerability Database (NVD) API
source.authority: Highest - Official US government CVE database

extract.field: references[] - Links to advisories and patches

source.rate_limit: 5 requests per 30 seconds without API key
source.best_practice: Parse JSON response to extract vulnerability details programmatically

source.name: GitHub Advisory Database API
source.authority: High - Aggregates security advisories from multiple sources

extract.field: severity - Severity level (critical, high, medium, low)

source.authentication: Optional GitHub token for higher rate limits
source.best_practice: Use ecosystem filter to narrow results to relevant packages

source.name: Official Vendor Security Advisory APIs
source.authority: Highest for specific packages - Direct from maintainers

example.name: npm Registry API
example.extract: Check "versions" object for security advisories in package metadata

example.name: Maven Central API
example.extract: Check for security metadata in response

source.name: OSV (Open Source Vulnerabilities) API
source.best_practice: Query by package name and version for precise results

source.name: Snyk Vulnerability Database API
source.note: Requires paid Snyk account for full API access

source.name: MITRE CVE API
source.best_practice: Use as fallback if NVD API is unavailable

information.question: RCE, XSS, SQL Injection, SSRF, Configuration issue, etc.?

category.name: dependency_vulnerability

indicators.indicator: CVE mentions package name and version

category.fix_strategy: Upgrade to patched version specified in advisory

category.name: code_pattern_vulnerability

indicators.indicator: CVE describes coding practice (SQLi, XSS, etc.)

research_focus.focus: OWASP guidelines for the vulnerability type
Secure coding patterns for the language/framework

category.fix_strategy: Refactor code to use secure alternatives

examples.example: Sanitize output, use Content Security Policy
Avoid shell execution, use safe APIs

indicators.indicator: CVE mentions configuration, secrets, or settings

research_focus.focus: Security best practices for the framework
Industry standards for the configuration type

examples.example: Move to environment variables, use secret managers

indicators.indicator: Issue describes container or infrastructure issue

cross_reference_validation.title: CRITICAL: Always Verify Across Multiple Sources Using APIs
cross_reference_validation.important_note: ALWAYS use API endpoints for CVE research instead of browser-based manual research. See 7_api_based_cve_research.xml for detailed API usage instructions and examples.

steps.step: Query NVD API for official CVE details
Query GitHub Advisory Database API for package-specific information
Query OSV API for cross-ecosystem vulnerability data
Validate that all API responses are consistent
Document all API queries and responses

cross_reference_validation.rationale: API-based research is faster, more accurate, and automatable compared to manual browser research. Different sources may have conflicting or incomplete information. Cross-referencing multiple APIs ensures you have accurate, complete data.
cross_reference_validation.reference: For complete API usage guide with examples, see: 7_api_based_cve_research.xml

research_documentation_template.title: Document Your Research

findings.finding: No breaking changes in patch version
Root cause: Unsafe deserialization in RSC Flight protocol

step.conclusion: Upgrade next from 16.0.6 to 16.0.7 (patch bump, no breaking changes)

mistake.name: using_browser_instead_of_apis
mistake.description: Manually browsing websites instead of using APIs for CVE research
mistake.why_wrong: Manual browser research is slow, error-prone, not automatable, and inconsistent
mistake.correct_approach: Always use API endpoints (NVD, GitHub Advisory, OSV) - see 7_api_based_cve_research.xml

mistake.name: relying_only_on_repository
mistake.description: Using only repository documentation without checking official CVE sources
mistake.why_wrong: Repository docs may be outdated or incorrect
mistake.correct_approach: Always verify with official CVE API databases first

mistake.name: assuming_generic_patterns
mistake.description: Assuming all dependency CVEs are fixed by simple upgrades
mistake.why_wrong: Some require code changes or configuration updates
mistake.correct_approach: Always check official advisory for specific guidance

mistake.name: ignoring_compatibility
mistake.description: Not checking if official fix works with current environment
mistake.why_wrong: Official fix may not be compatible with current dependencies

mistake.name: not_understanding_vulnerability
mistake.why_wrong: May miss edge cases or introduce new issues
mistake.correct_approach: Always research the root cause and attack vector

red_flags_requiring_clarification.title: STOP and Seek Clarification If:
red_flags_requiring_clarification.red_flag: Official advisory conflicts with repository documentation
Patched version incompatible with current dependencies
Multiple conflicting fix recommendations from different sources
No clear patched version specified in advisories
Fix requires extensive code refactoring without clear guidance
Downgrade seems necessary (very rare, usually wrong)

validation_checklist.title: Before Implementing ANY Fix

checklist.item: ✅ Researched CVE using APIs (NVD API, GitHub Advisory API, OSV API)
✅ Documented all API queries and responses
✅ Identified exact vulnerability type and category
✅ Found official patched version or secure coding pattern
✅ Cross-referenced information across multiple API sources
✅ Validated that all APIs return consistent information
✅ Validated fix compatibility with current repository state
✅ Checked for breaking changes and migration requirements
✅ Identified all breaking changes in the fix
✅ Documented all research sources and decision rationale

validation_checklist.reference: See 7_api_based_cve_research.xml for API usage examples and automation scripts
